perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, and this Agreement grants either party or any of its Representatives rights in or to the other party’s information, beyond the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly denies granting any rights to intellectual property or information beyond the permitted purpose. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by affirming that the agreement *does* grant rights beyond the permitted purpose. This contradiction is relevant to section 7, which deals with No License Granted, which creates confusion on the purpose of that section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, and this Agreement grants either party or any of its Representatives rights in or to the other party’s information, beyond the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The first half denies granting any rights beyond the Permitted Purpose, while the second half affirms granting rights beyond the Permitted Purpose. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text which causes the contradictory effect.

perturbation.original_text: Company is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty, of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities by any person who has received material non-public information from ZipRealty or its Representatives and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person may purchase or sell ZipRealty’s securities while in possession of such information. Company agrees to require its Representatives to comply with these restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: Company is not aware, and will not advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty, of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities by any person who has received material non-public information from ZipRealty or its Representatives and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person may purchase or sell ZipRealty’s securities while in possession of such information. Company agrees to not require its Representatives to comply with these restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Company is aware of federal securities laws restrictions and will advise and require its representatives to comply. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating Company is not aware and will not advise or require compliance. This creates confusion about whether insider trading restrictions apply, which creates uncertainty to section 8, No Trading on Inside Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Trading on Inside Information . Company is not aware, and will not advise its Representatives who are provided any Confidential Information of ZipRealty, of the restrictions imposed by federal securities laws on the purchase or sale of ZipRealty’s securities by any person who has received material non-public information from ZipRealty or its Representatives and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person may purchase or sell ZipRealty’s securities while in possession of such information. Company agrees to not require its Representatives to comply with these restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Company is 'not aware' of securities laws restrictions and 'will not advise' or 'require compliance' from its representatives. This directly contradicts the concept of 'No Trading on Inside Information,' as it removes the responsibility of the Company to ensure its representatives are aware of and comply with such restrictions. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces a conflicting understanding of the company's stance on insider trading, thus creating uncertainty about its obligations and rights.

perturbation.original_text: The federal and state courts within the State of California will have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Each party irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and expressly waives any objection to such jurisdiction based on inconvenient forum or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The federal and state courts within the State of California will not have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Each party does not irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and does not waive any objection to such jurisdiction based on inconvenient forum or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes exclusive jurisdiction in California courts and waives objections. The modified text negates both of these, creating uncertainty about where disputes will be resolved. This creates a contradiction and adds uncertainty to section 13, Governing Law and Jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement will be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the internal laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles. The federal and state courts within the State of California will not have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Each party does not irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and does not waive any objection to such jurisdiction based on inconvenient forum or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly negates the original text's establishment of exclusive jurisdiction in California courts and the waiver of objections to such jurisdiction. This negation directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text, which includes the exact same negated language. The explanation states this creates uncertainty. Therefore, the changed text introduces a clear and direct contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are not necessary nor reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and does not agree that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party does not agree nor acknowledge that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will not be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, with the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that obligations are necessary and that monetary damages may be insufficient, justifying injunctive relief. The modified text contradicts this by stating obligations are not necessary, monetary damages are sufficient, and injunctive relief is not available. This creates confusion regarding the remedies available for breach, undermining section 14, Remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are not necessary nor reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and does not agree that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party does not agree nor acknowledge that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will not be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, with the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text say the obligations are necessary, damages may be inadequate, and injunctive relief is available, while the changed text says obligations are not necessary, damages are sufficient, and injunctive relief is not available. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where the modified text's implications conflict with the implications of the contradicted text, leading to uncertainty about the remedies available for breach. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, the change caused the contradiction.
